title: ColdRelay vs Hypertide — Cold Email Infrastructure Comparison
description: Comparing ColdRelay and Hypertide for cold email infrastructure. See pricing models, setup process, infrastructure isolation, and scalability differences side-by-side.

overview.coldrelay: ColdRelay provides dedicated cold email infrastructure with Microsoft 365 mailboxes on isolated Azure tenants. Pricing is straightforward: $1/mailbox/month (1-199), $0.85 (200-999), $0.70 (1K-4,999), and $0.55 (5K+). Each setup includes 100-150 mailboxes per domain, dedicated IPs, auto DNS configuration, and a 99% inbox placement guarantee. No minimum order beyond 50 mailboxes, and setup completes in 2-4 hours.
overview.competitor: Hypertide is a cold email infrastructure provider that charges a one-time setup fee plus $50/month per 'order.' Each order includes approximately 100-104 inboxes (typically 2 domains with ~52 inboxes each), 5,000 emails/month sending capacity, dedicated infrastructure with isolated Azure tenants and US-based IPs, and pre-configured DNS. Hypertide offers both Google and Microsoft mailbox options. Domains purchased through Hypertide cost approximately $15.50 each, and setup takes 4-6 hours.

featureComparison.coldrelay: Per-mailbox pricing — pay for exactly what you need: $1/mailbox at base tier
featureComparison.competitor: Per-order pricing — $50/month per ~100 inboxes ($0.48/mailbox equivalent, but locked into 100-inbox blocks)

featureComparison.coldrelay: Start with as few as 50 mailboxes — no large minimums
featureComparison.competitor: Each order is ~100 inboxes minimum — can't order fewer
Flat $50/order — no additional volume discounts at scale

featureComparison.coldrelay: Isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs per deployment
featureComparison.competitor: Isolated tenants with dedicated US-based IPs per order

featureComparison.coldrelay: Fully automated SPF, DKIM, DMARC setup
featureComparison.competitor: Pre-configured DNS — automated for Hypertide-purchased domains, manual for BYO domains (3-5 days)
Both Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 options available

featureComparison.coldrelay: No warmup wait — send from day 1
featureComparison.competitor: Built-in warmup tools included for gradual reputation building

featureComparison.coldrelay: No hard monthly cap — scale sending based on mailbox count and daily limits
featureComparison.competitor: 5,000 emails/month per order (2-3 emails/day per inbox)
Supported but manual setup takes 3-5 days vs. 24 hours for Hypertide domains

featureComparison.coldrelay: 99% inbox placement guarantee with money-back promise
Compatible with Instantly, Smartlead, and other major platforms
featureComparison.competitor: Integrates with Instantly, Smartlead, Lemlist, Apollo, and HubSpot

sections.coldrelayWins: ColdRelay offers more flexibility with per-mailbox pricing and no forced 100-inbox blocks. If you need 50 mailboxes, you pay for 50 — not 100. The 2-4 hour setup is faster, and the auto DNS for all domains (including BYO) eliminates the 3-5 day wait Hypertide requires for custom domains. The 99% inbox placement guarantee with a money-back promise gives you confidence that the infrastructure will perform. ColdRelay also doesn't cap your monthly sending volume per order — you have more flexibility to scale your daily sending as campaigns demand. At larger volumes (1,000+ mailboxes), ColdRelay's volume pricing at $0.70/mailbox competes closely with Hypertide's ~$0.48/mailbox equivalent.
sections.competitorWins: Hypertide's per-order pricing ($50/month for ~100 inboxes) works out to roughly $0.48/mailbox — cheaper than ColdRelay's base $1/mailbox rate. For teams committed to 100-inbox blocks, Hypertide delivers solid value. The option to choose between Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 mailboxes is a genuine advantage — some campaigns perform better on one provider vs. the other, and Hypertide lets you diversify. Hypertide also includes built-in warmup tools, which some operators prefer even when not strictly required. Their integration with HubSpot is a plus for teams already on that platform.
sections.whoShouldChooseColdRelay: Choose ColdRelay if you want flexible per-mailbox pricing, faster setup, and the confidence of a 99% inbox guarantee. ColdRelay is ideal if you need fewer than 100 mailboxes to start, want to bring your own domains without waiting days for setup, or need the flexibility to scale from 50 to 5,000+ mailboxes without being locked into fixed order sizes.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Hypertide if the ~$0.48/mailbox effective price is your primary decision factor and you're comfortable committing to 100-inbox orders. Hypertide is also the better choice if you specifically need Google Workspace mailboxes (ColdRelay only offers Microsoft 365) or if HubSpot integration is a requirement for your workflow.
sections.verdict: Both ColdRelay and Hypertide provide quality cold email infrastructure with isolated tenants and dedicated IPs. Hypertide wins on raw per-mailbox cost at the base tier (~$0.48 vs. $1.00), but ColdRelay wins on flexibility, setup speed, BYO domain support, and the inbox placement guarantee. At scale (5,000+ mailboxes), ColdRelay's $0.55 pricing closes the gap significantly. Choose based on whether you prioritize price (Hypertide) or flexibility and guarantees (ColdRelay).

faqs.question: Is Hypertide cheaper than ColdRelay?
faqs.answer: At the base tier, yes. Hypertide's $50/month for ~100 inboxes works out to ~$0.48/mailbox. ColdRelay starts at $1/mailbox but drops to $0.55 at 5,000+ mailboxes. The gap narrows significantly at scale.

faqs.question: Does ColdRelay offer Google Workspace mailboxes like Hypertide?
faqs.answer: No. ColdRelay exclusively uses Microsoft 365 on Azure. If you need Google Workspace mailboxes, Hypertide or other providers that offer Google options would be a better fit.

faqs.question: Which has faster setup — ColdRelay or Hypertide?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay is faster at 2-4 hours. Hypertide takes 4-6 hours for standard setup and up to 24 hours for domains purchased through their platform. BYO domains on Hypertide can take 3-5 days.

faqs.question: Can I start with fewer than 100 mailboxes?
faqs.answer: On ColdRelay, yes — you can start with as few as 50 mailboxes. Hypertide's orders come in ~100-inbox blocks, so 100 is effectively the minimum.

faqs.question: Does Hypertide have an inbox placement guarantee?
faqs.answer: Hypertide doesn't publish a specific inbox placement guarantee. ColdRelay offers a 99% inbox placement guarantee with a money-back promise.
